The girl's mother continues to defend her, until events take a dramatic turn

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

The youngsters involved in the earlier case escaped - though their abductor was never found. Fearing they may be on the trail of a serial killer, the detectives also uncover a link with another unsolved case - the assault on a champion fisherman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry's suspicions about the death of CIA boss Samuel Walker are confirmed when three more intelligence officers are killed - fatalities that coincide with the re-emergence of Ros's former MI6 mentor, who asks her to read his memoirs. Finding inconsistencies in the retired agent's recollections, she begins to suspect a terrible plot has been put into action and risks her life to discover the truth. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty

Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: MOBO Awards 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the 2020 MOBO Awards, a virtual celebration of black music and culture. This year's unique ceremony involves ten awards, two cultural films and nine unique performances from some of the biggest and most exciting names in music right now, all unified with a positive narrative of mobilisation after one of the most extraordinary years in modern history - from the global Covid-19 pandemic, to the public killing of George Floyd and the subsequent rise to prominence of the Black Lives Matter. Featuring performances by Headie One and M Huncho, Ms Banks, Tiwa Savage and Kojey Radical

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Sam, who has Down's syndrome, prepares for his debut in a public wrestling match. Marcus is enjoying success in the ring, and is pursuing romance outside it

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV exposes a chip shop worker's self-inflicted personal injury, a jewellery scam is exposed, and a motorist claiming against a bus company ends up regretting his road rage

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A policeman saves residents from fire, a couple on a mission to save rhinos end up needing saving themselves and a woman is rescued after sailing into stormy seas

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at the helm of a festive edition from the Newark Antiques fair. Experts Kate Bliss and Thomas Forrester help the reds and blues go head to head to find some yuletide bargains that they hope will make a profit at auction. Meanwhile, Anita takes a look at our Christmas television viewing habits of years gone by

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma tries to hide her little secret, while Valerie supports Lily through a tough time. Bear learns a lesson about love from a homeless artist

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple plan to spend £750,000 on a home in South Devon large enough to accommodate their library of more than 3,000 books, and Margherita Taylor visits Agatha Christie's home

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform, including a chest of drawers and a collection of redundant file boxes

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet continue their search in Bristol and Gloucestershire, and while Serhat aims for glass and metalware, Christina causes chaos trying to get her hands on possibly the largest Road Trip item ever

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The investigation into Ian's attack continues but as alibis are given, other information comes to light giving the police more leads. Kat is stunned when Jack tells her about Kush's visit to the police station. Jay continues to support Honey in the aftermath of her assault, and Suki tries to make an example of Will for shoplifting

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second group of four face two tough tests in a bid to make it through to finals week. First they receive a masterclass with Michelin-starred chef Andrew Wong, who demonstrates two dishes - dim sum and crispy chilli beef. They must then produce two dishes, emulating Andrew's techniques, with the judges' favourite receiving a pass through to next week. the remaining three must serve up a two-course signature menu, after which Monica Galetti, Gregg Wallace and Marcus Wareing will decide who else deserves a place in the final

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The redevelopment of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, a semi-detached house in Bloxwich in the West Midlands and a grade II listed building in Milton Regis, Kent

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Insurers turn to undercover filming to get to the bottom of a suspicious personal injury claim, a scam to cash in on a mobile phone lost in a toilet goes down the pan and social media is the undoing of an opportunist as he boxes himself into a corner. An audacious claim for compensation ends up costing one man a lot more than he bargained for when CCTV shows what really happened, and in the USA a missing person investigation turns out to be an elaborate fake death scam to cash in on a life insurance policy

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at the best entertainment items bought by past teams, and watches some very famous theatre props fly at auction

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to south Wales with a couple who have spent three decades in military housing, and are looking for their very own country home on a budget of £430,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet head to south Wales. There's a mad dash around the shops as Serhat tries to eke out his tiny budget, while Christina struggles to blow all of her money. They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jordan Banjo present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Amy Fuller, world indoor champion sprinter Richard Kitty and netball star Pamela Cookey join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the search for Ian's attacker continues, a police investigation gets under way, while Jack has a different suspect in his sights and others try to cover their tracks. Callum continues in his own questioning - but could his answers be closer to home?

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the quiz, inviting panellists to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: The morning of the wedding arrives and it is not just Denise's nerves that are on edge. Jim has diarrhoea and a constipated Nana warns the Royle bride that it's all downhill from here

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actresses Gal Gadot and Tessa Thompson, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman, whose debut book Quite is all about 'love, life and eyeliner'. Derry Girls star Nicola Coughlan chats about her involvement in American period drama Bridgerton and comedian Lee Mack discusses the return of his comedy series Not Going Out. With music from McFly, who perform their current single Tonight Is the Night

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens continue their first Drag Race UK tour. It's an all-ages show in Glasgow and Alyssa Edwards is moved, while Crystal's death-defying act causes a medical emergency for a member of the audience

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens conclude their inaugural Drag Race UK tour as, on the final night, Secret Santa comes to town and it looks like she's bearing gifts for those participants who have been more naughty than nice

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a brand new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Porcelain dealer Serhat Ahmet and jewellery expert Christina Trevanion play to their strengths in the antiques shops of the West Midlands. Plus, an insight to Tudor cleanliness

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian semi in London's Forest Hill, a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and a piece of land in Staffordshire

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working at Britain's biggest operational nuclear site at Heysham on the Lancashire coast. Heysham 1 and Heysham 2 power stations make enough electricity to power more than four million homes. Keeley follows engineers as they undertake a huge maintenance programme called an outage. But with Britain in lockdown during the coronavirus pandemic will engineers be able to get one of the reactors back online to provide much-needed energy to the nation?

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Rebecca Jones introduces her class of eight autistic boys, each with their own individual needs, including Ethan - who likes to answer the school's telephone. Fifteen-year-old Luke was born with schizencephaly - a hole in his brain - which has left him with long-term physical health problems. Ysgol y Deri is his lifeline and with voice-generating software, teachers are helping him to express his emotions

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Contestants include journalism student Charlotte and retired police officer Andrew, along with five-time Head Hunter Dubem, who answer questions on rugby union and religion

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Denise Lewis joins the duo as they create a trio of canapes, porcetta pork loin and a prawn and salmon terrine. The retired athlete also explains how she spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun there are also fantastic ideas for homemade edible Christmas presents

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The cook sets out to prove that healthy food can taste great as he prepares a week's worth of sumptuous dishes from seasonal produce. He also tidies his garden and makes plans for the growing season ahead

programme.title: The Pure Hell of St Trinian's (1960)
programme.desc: The girls burn their school to the ground, prompting a mysterious Middle Eastern gentleman to offer new premises for their studies - but he has a sinister ulterior motive for helping the badly behaved youngsters. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, Joyce Grenfell, George Cole and Irene Handl

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores animals that have developed tool-making skills to help them survive, including orang-utans and crows. He also examines two families with remarkable powers of regenerating unusually large body parts easily - salamanders that do so when a tail or a limb has been bitten off by a predator, and male moose that grow and shed antlers every year

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition, travelling up and down the county revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, with new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin tells the incredible story of the Wagoners and their role in the First World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual go test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women co-hosts Nadia Sawalha and Kaye Adams join forces with experts Tim Medhurst and Anita Manning in the antique shops of Edinburgh and beyond. Nadia picks up quite the menagerie of antique animals, including three monkeys and a reindeer, while Kaye gambles a large chunk of her £400 on an art deco bracelet. An unsavoury incident at the auction and an accusation of foul play makes for a tense finale

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the fish and main heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. For the fish course, there is salmon and turbot, cooked in a giant antique copper pan known as a turbot kettle. For the main course, dishes include venison and a roast that combines 12 different birds. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. As Blayne tightens his grip on power, Anne is under pressure, but at what price? North is given valuable information by Lady Leadsom, and Kate attempts to gain the trust of the prince. Starring Lesley Manville and Liv Tyler

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended version of the quiz in which Jason Manford, Romesh Ranganathan and Holly Walsh join regular participant Alan Davies to vie for points awarded for interesting - if not always correct - answers. In a Christmas-themed edition, the panellists learn the best way to wrap an awkwardly shaped present and get wine stains out of a carpet

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A bed shortage in Barnet hospital highlights the perpetual challenge of trying to discharge older patients, alongside the added complexities of Covid-19 infection risks. As autumn begins, the hospital's emergency department is seeing a resurgence of patients after a summer of low attendances, with more people arriving in A&E than almost any other day since last winter, and they are admitting more patients than they are discharging

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, pubs have been closing down at an alarming rate. Concerned by a crisis in the industry he loves, Tom Kerridge sets out on a mission to revive struggling pubs. He begins at the White Hart in Chilsworthy, Cornwall, where a 300-year-old freehouse, the only pub in the village, is in danger of closing

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The third lot of quarter-finalists are challenged to create a dish that makes great use of citrus, presented with an array of fruits from kumquats to lemons, as well as other ingredients. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Jay Rayner and Tracey MacLeod, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe and Cameron are driven further apart when they end up competing for the same job - and tension rises even further when Cameron deliberately sabotages his rival's chances. Skyler, Jeong and Josh work together as a team to save a gravely ill patient, under Henrik's watchful eye. Josh begins to see there is more to Skyler than he gave her credit for. Sacha and Jodie band together after their explosive domestic outburst

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Exeter's Westpoint Arena and tries his hand at basket-weaving on the Somerset levels. With experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet go out of their comfort zones in their search for old treasures, hitting the shops in Sheffield and Coventry

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Cameras also follow great dane Shelby as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Anna, Victoria and Jordon must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create a Turkey jambalaya, a spicy lamb shank and a madeira cake with a chocolate, chilli and ginger fondue, as they find out how guest Nicki Chapman spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun, there are also fantastic ideas for home-made edible Christmas presents

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode presents festive recipes from the archives by a number of TV chefs, including a Christmas cake by Fanny Cradock. Plus, Lorraine Pascale reveals how to prepare a turkey

programme.title: The Lavender Hill Mob (1951)
programme.desc: An unassuming bank worker masterminds a plot to steal £1million in gold bullion, then melts it down and turns it into statues of the Eiffel Tower that can easily be exported - but the plan soon goes awry, with hilarious results. Ealing comedy, starring Alec Guinness, Stanley Holloway, Sid James and Audrey Hepburn

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores nature's evolutionary anomalies, beginning by examining how the unique features of the chameleon and the giraffe have benefited them in their respective environments. He then looks at curious creatures that have led to accusations of forgery, such as the duck-billed platypus and the midwife toad

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, alongside experts James Lewis and Elizabeth Talbot. Featuring a Hampshire sculptor who carves giant spiders

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Weekday fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring interviews with the couples and a sneak peek at what they will be wearing on Saturday with designer Vicky Gill

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actresses Sue Johnston and Lisa Riley join forces with experts Catherine Southon and David Harper to visit antique shops in the north west of England. Sue doles out more than half her £400 budget in her very first shop, while Lisa adopts a more conservative approach. An eclectic mix of collectables attract the celebrities, but will they appeal to the bidders at auction?

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Shih tzu-pug cross Miska moves on to a houseboat with new owner Scarlett. After signs that Miska is adapting well to her new home, Scarlett takes her to work, but she is shocked when the dog ignores her and starts yapping at her colleagues in the open-plan office. In Surrey, the Summerscale-Heards continue training St Bernard pup Woody to offer assistance to five-year-old Dexter, and in West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo joins retirees Linda and Andrew - only to start nipping and jumping up

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The second documentary looks at how Britain coped with the loss of empire, the dominance of America and the rise of a consolidated European superstate in the decades after the Second World War. Andrew Marr profiles some of the key figures who sought to come to terms with Britain's decline, including Earl Mountbatten of Burma, cookery writer Elizabeth David and singer and activist Bob Geldof, as well as less familiar figures like Colonel Colin 'Mad Mitch' Mitchell, and pioneering peace campaigner Helen John

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Laura Lexx and Glenn Moore. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that happened in the week the programme first aired in June 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Beguiled (2017)
programme.desc: The arrival of a wounded Civil War soldier brings jealousy and betrayal to a Southern girls' school. Period drama, starring Colin Farrell, Nicole Kidman and Kirsten Dunst

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Clara Amfo and Dev Griffin to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first series of Strictly Come Dancing and the final episodes of Friends and Sex and the City

programme.title: Lockdown 1.0 - Following the Science?
programme.desc: An investigation into just how closely Boris Johnson and the Government followed the scientific evidence throughout the early months of the coronavirus pandemic. Scientists at the heart of Britain's response to the virus reveal what happened behind the scenes, and how problems accessing key data caused flaws in the modelling process that meant fighting the virus was an uphill battle from the very beginning

programme.title: 25 Siblings & Me
programme.desc: The incredible story of Oli, a young man with Asperger's syndrome, who overnight discovered he has 25 siblings from the same American sperm donor dad. Desperate to be part of this new family, Oli travels to the USA, meeting his donor and joining a mass sibling reunion

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet continue their search in Bristol and Gloucestershire, and while Serhat aims for glass and metalware, Christina causes chaos trying to get her hands on possibly the largest Road Trip item ever

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival of the day is widow Eve Robinson from Kings Lynn, Norfolk, who hopes that goldsmith Richard Talman can mend her diamond wedding ring. Next, Wizard Geoff turns his attention to a vintage penny arcade game which was salvaged from a bonfire over 40 years ago. Elsewhere, the experts set to work repairing a shop mannequin and a pair of lumberjack's tree spurs

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create dishes for unexpected guests, with impressionist Jon Culshaw joining them to make a last-minute Christmas cake, and orange and honey glazed salmon

programme.title: Nigel Slater's 12 Tastes of Christmas
programme.desc: The food writer presents the first of two programmes in which he creates a selection of festive dishes, getting into the spirit of the season with ideas for both self-indulgence and entertaining. Nigel also makes a culinary trip to Norway, during which he goes in search of a special Christmas tree

programme.title: Lucky Jim (1957)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking, unconventional junior lecturer is frustrated with his stuffy university, but still wants to curry favour with the formidable head of the history department. A family gathering and an academic ceremony provide opportunities to make an impression - but nothing goes to plan. Comedy based on Kingsley Amis's novel, starring Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas and Hugh Griffith

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates how nature has tinkered with the ageing process to different effect, and profiles evolutionary anomalies. He recounts how scientists are beginning to unravel the true nature of the narwhal's tusk and the reason for its spiral shape. The spiral is also vital to the lives of snails, allowing them to leave the sea and conquer land.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the RAF Museum at Hendon in London, antiques experts Anita Manning and Mark Stacey find some real treasures among the bags and boxes to take to auction. There's a photo album of the facility in the '30s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: All the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as the contenders prepare for tomorrow night's show. Including reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Martel Maxwell compete in the money-making challenge, scouring the antiques shops of Cheshire with the assistance of experts Roo Irvine and James Braxton. Former soldier Rav fancies some militaria, while Martel reveals a fine ability to hold a tune and indulges her musical side with the purchase of a grand piano. But which one will have shown to have played to their strengths at the auction in Bolton?

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: During the hottest time of year, the waterhole becomes busier in the cool of night. Using remote cameras, the team uncovers the new dynamics of the period following sunset. Hyena move into the area, but they are so elusive that it is hard to know how big the clan is. Their arrival has a dramatic effect on other predators in the area, and no lions or leopards are spotted while they are around. Ella Al-Shamahi discovers how the increased footfall has changed the shape of the waterhole and how, as temperatures rise, the waterhole residents' behaviour is also changing

programme.title: The Mercy (2017)
programme.desc: The story of amateur sailor Donald Crowhurst and his solo attempt to circumnavigate the globe in 1968. The struggles he confronted on the controversial voyage, while his family awaited his return, is one of the most enduring mysteries of recent times. Factual drama, starring David Thewlis and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul travels to the very north of the British Isles, to The Shetland Isles - islands which are as Scandinavian as they are British. During his northerly adventures, Paul lands on the island of Yell, where he meets with local Brydon Thomason, from Shetland Nature, and gets the amazing opportunity to see wild otters up close.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories. With Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: This winter sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin, alongside paintings by Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum next spring. This documentary charts the affinities between Munch's art and Emin's, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britain's pre-eminent artists

programme.title: The Hurt Locker (2008)
programme.desc: Two American bomb disposal experts operating in Iraq are assigned a new leader after their previous one dies in action. However, the sergeant's reckless attitude and love of battle consistently puts them at risk. Kathryn Bigelow's Oscar-winning drama, starring Jeremy Renner, Anthony Mackie, Guy Pearce and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Pegasus' commander Fisk is murdered following Roslin's pledge to stamp out the fleet's black market and introduce new trade regulations. Lee is charged with solving the crime but finds himself in danger when his investigation leads deep into military, civilian and government circles. Sci-fi drama, with Jamie Bamber and Mary McDonnell

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The team are called into action to investigate a Colonial mining operation. Meanwhile, an implacable Cylon raider sets out on a destructive rampage, carrying out a series of hit-and-run attacks on the fleet. The exhausted Viper pilots have no choice but to do battle with their formidable foe, whom they nickname Scar

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the third round of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. This episode sees Actors Monica Dolan and Bob Barrett, representing Guildhall School of Music and Drama, in a festive contest with presenter Maryam Moshiri and Arctic explorer Pen Hadow, both former students at UCL

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold examine the festive season as it would have been celebrated in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII, concentrating on Christmas Day, New Year's Day and Twelfth Night. They make Tudor decorations, engage in festive revels and prepare feasting delights such as boar's head and shred pies - which were forerunners of mince pies and were made with meat. They also investigate the Tudor origins of Christmas carols, the singing of which was known as wassailing, and learn about the Lord of Misrule - traditionally a commoner placed in authority over his social betters for the festive period and in charge of directing the Christmas revelry

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer addresses the history and use of music in television, beginning by examining the enduring power of the theme tune. On the streets of Coronation Street, he encounters a brass band playing the music that has announced the start of each episode since it began. Following the trail of the soap opera world, Neil meets composer Simon May, creator of the EastEnders theme tune, and travels to the US to talk with Matt Groening, creator of The Simpsons, to uncover how its orchestrated theme music is a homage to classic TV of the past. Themes to Z Cars, Bagpuss, Game of Thrones, Mastermind and Grandstand are also celebrated

programme.title: Make! Craft Britain
programme.desc: The novice crafters discover the ancient art of silver jewellery making, and origami expert Sam Tsang demonstrates how to make a snack box

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold examine the festive season as it would have been celebrated in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII, concentrating on Christmas Day, New Year's Day and Twelfth Night. They make Tudor decorations, engage in festive revels and prepare feasting delights such as boar's head and shred pies - which were forerunners of mince pies and were made with meat. They also investigate the Tudor origins of Christmas carols, the singing of which was known as wassailing, and learn about the Lord of Misrule - traditionally a commoner placed in authority over his social betters for the festive period and in charge of directing the Christmas revelry

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts as a team from the University of Liverpool, including actress Anna Maxwell Martin and broadcaster Roger Bolton, faces a team from Hull, with comedian Lucy Beaumont and novelist Tracy Borman

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates the creation of the perfect brown-toned scene of a mountain at sunset

programme.title: Brooklyn (2015)
programme.desc: An Irish woman living with her mother in the 1950s decides to make a fresh start in America. She makes her home in New York, and falls in love. However, her past catches up with her when she receives bad news from Ireland, and faces a harsh choice between her two homes. Drama based on Colm Toibin's novel, starring Saoirse Ronan, Domhnall Gleeson, Jim Broadbent and Julie Walters

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores some of the most spectacular and scary sea cliffs in Britain

programme.title: Attenborough at 90
programme.desc: Kirsty Young talks to David Attenborough about his life and career, discussing his travels around the world and most memorable encounters with wildlife. The programme features footage from his many natural history programmes, including scenes where unusual animal behaviour was caught on camera for the first time, and celebrates his influence on generations of viewers' understanding of the natural world. Featuring contributions from friends and colleagues including Michael Palin and Chris Packham

programme.title: A Fanatic Heart: Geldof on Yeats
programme.desc: The musician and campaigner examines the life and work of poet WB Yeats, arguing that he was a key figure in a cultural revolution and that his writing played a major role in Ireland's struggle for independence. Featuring readings of Yeats' verse by Bill Nighy, Van Morrison, Colin Farrell, Bono, Edna O'Brien, Ardal O'Hanlon, Noel Gallagher and Liam Neeson

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates the creation of the perfect brown-toned scene of a mountain at sunset

programme.title: Female Filmmakers: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez introduces a selection of short films made by women, telling stories from a contemporary female perspective

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Distinguished graduates of Royal Holloway, University of London and the University of Sussex battle for a place in the semi-finals. Among those competing are ceramics designer Emma Bridgewater, TV historian Fern Riddell, BBC Parliamentary correspondent Mark D'Arcy and sci-fi writer and critic Kim Newman. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of composer Vaughan Williams' The Lark Ascending, which was written in 1914 as Europe approached the First World War. The programme culminates in a performance by 15-year-old violinist Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews at Shirehampton Public Hall, Bristol, where the piece was premiered in December 1920. Featuring contributions by actor Peter Sallis, performer Tasmin Little, and critic and friend of the composer, Michael Kennedy

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on May 10, with performances by artists including Kylie Minogue, the Wonderstuff, Mantronix featuring Wondress, New Kids on the Block, Michael Bolton, En Vogue, Thunder, Adamski and Beats International

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on May 18, with performances by artists including Hothouse Flowers, the Pasadenas, the Chimes, Sam Brown, Adamski, Depeche Mode, Eurythmics and Paul Young

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: With the advent of commercial television in the UK, ITV rivalled the BBC for airtime and lured viewers in with the new language of advertisements. However, these had not yet reached the impact they had seen in the USA, the home of the TV jingle, where a 30-second tune could make or break a brand, as seen in the competing fortunes of Coca-Cola and Pepsi. Neil reveals the lasting musical power of jingles, idents and advertisements, including the long-forgotten test card tunes sampled in American hip-hop

programme.title: Dionne Warwick at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances by the US singer, one of the most-charted female vocalists of all time

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The singer looks back on her 50-year career, which began with the release in 1962 of her first single Don't Make Me Over and has most recently seen her re-record some of her best-known hits. The entertainer also discusses the death of her cousin Whitney Houston earlier this year, fighting racism, and the importance of her Christian faith

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by prominent soul artists and groups of the decade, including Aretha Franklin, Billy Preston, the Tams, Curtis Mayfield, Bill Withers, the Stylistics, Gil Scott-Heron and the Jacksons

programme.title: Bros: After the Screaming Stops (2018)
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s Bros were one of the biggest bands in the world. Having sold out stadiums, they were the youngest to this day to play Wembley Stadium and their Push album went to Number 1 in more than 20 countries, selling 10 million copies worldwide. After three years of hysteria wherever twins Matt and Luke Goss went, this documentary recalls the aftermath - the fragility of fame and the heartbreak that followed. It also charts the pair's professional reunion 28 years on, having hardly spoken and not played together since their split, as they prepare to play London's O2

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Music compilation show celebrating some of the most popular boy bands to have graced TV screens since the 1960s. The programme highlights performances by acts defined by vocal harmonies, synchronised dance-steps and character appeal, including JLS, the Four Tops, the Monkees, Westlife, Village People and Blazin' Squad

programme.title: The Lark Ascending

programme.desc: Pablo thinks he'll float away if he puts down his pine cones. Draff disagrees, but when Pablo puts them down, he floats up into the sky and his friends have to rescue him

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a textile airport and a star kaleidoscope, and watch as Book Boy makes a trapeze.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Isla and Noah pack their suitcases and go on a family holiday to the seaside

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Dad takes Tig to the swing park and she sees her friend playing with children she does not know. The little girl feels nervous, but Woolly gives her the confidence to join them

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: It's Gurdy's lots-of-birthdays-birthday

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Choekyi's Momos are a type of dumpling filled with spinach, cheese and mushrooms

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The friends become obsessed with triangles, but they soon realise circles are very useful too

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: It is Hogmanay on the island, and the little girl awaits the arrival of her parents and new baby sister. But a storm has delayed their ferry, and they might miss the celebrations

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: The little girl is inspired by her art gallery visit with Mum and listens to Carnival of Animals, The Swan by Camille Saint-Saens while she uses her imagination

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It's big bop night at the Alphadrome and bossy bass-player B pretends to be drummer D

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘rocket' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five arrives to get the band together

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny falls in love with a lost dog he and Flop find in the park and decides he wants to keep her. When the real owner turns up, Bing is very upset when it comes to saying goodbye to his new friend

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy's thirsty for an adventure, so he goes to Okido to help his friends solve the mystery of the dried-up Oasis

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are outside in the cold when they spot the Tiddlytubbies who are warm, inside. The Teletubbies put on hats, gloves and scarves to keep warm

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Team Tinpo are called out to Tinpotown Airfield, where their effort to get things off the ground inadvertently kicks off a bird b-boy party

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog Loves Bedtime Baboon? Dog and Pug go on an adventure to help Bedtime Baboon.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits Sebastian and his guinea pig Bonbon who has a lump on her leg that needs checking by a vet. Ferne goes to meet a vet who is caring for a badger at a wildlife hospital

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Maia has a big family weekend planned. First, she visits her baby cousin Jessica. They enjoy playing peek-a-boo together and Maia even helps to feed her. Later, Dad's family arrive for a traditional Zambian meal. Maia helps prepare the food with her Aunty and then they all dress up in special Zambian clothes to eat

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how a window cleaning cradle works and finds out how shadows are made.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbit accidentally brings home Mr MacGregor's cat instead of a cart of Brussels sprouts, and must find a way to lure her into the garden safely

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It’s Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won’t be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren’t fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are really little!

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day heads to Ethiopia in Africa in search of an Ethiopian wolf pack. Along the way, he meets some bearded vultures and comes face to face with a family of hamadryas baboons

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are watching for polar bears, but an iceberg is heading for their ship

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Mr Bouncer gets too sick to make the important holiday supply delivery, Peter and Benjamin brave a blizzard to get the job done and meet a new friend along the way

programme.title: Moon and Me

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.desc: Upsy Daisy sings with a megaphone in the garden, and wants to carry on instead of going to bed or playing with Igglepiggle. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: MOBO Awards 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the 2020 MOBO Awards, a virtual celebration of black music and culture. This year's unique ceremony involves ten awards, two cultural films and nine unique performances from some of the biggest and most exciting names in music right now, all unified with a positive narrative of mobilisation after one of the most extraordinary years in modern history - from the global Covid-19 pandemic, to the public killing of George Floyd and the subsequent rise to prominence of the Black Lives Matter. Featuring performances by Headie One and M Huncho, Ms Banks, Tiwa Savage and Kojey Radical

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second group of four face two tough tests in a bid to make it through to finals week. First they receive a masterclass with Michelin-starred chef Andrew Wong, who demonstrates two dishes - dim sum and crispy chilli beef. They must then produce two dishes, emulating Andrew's techniques, with the judges' favourite receiving a pass through to next week. the remaining three must serve up a two-course signature menu, after which Monica Galetti, Gregg Wallace and Marcus Wareing will decide who else deserves a place in the final

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at the best entertainment items bought by past teams, and watches some very famous theatre props fly at auction

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to south Wales with a couple who have spent three decades in military housing, and are looking for their very own country home on a budget of £430,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Serhat Ahmet head to south Wales. There's a mad dash around the shops as Serhat tries to eke out his tiny budget, while Christina struggles to blow all of her money. They look at baths, buckets and big sticks to try to turn a profit at the auction. Serhat follows in the footsteps of two brothers who discovered a whole underground world beneath their farmland, while a trip to a spa doesn't turn out quite as expected for Christina as she takes the plunge into a rather weird and wacky sport

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the search for Ian's attacker continues, a police investigation gets under way, while Jack has a different suspect in his sights and others try to cover their tracks. Callum continues in his own questioning - but could his answers be closer to home?

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the quiz, inviting panellists to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actresses Gal Gadot and Tessa Thompson, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman, whose debut book Quite is all about 'love, life and eyeliner'. Derry Girls star Nicola Coughlan chats about her involvement in American period drama Bridgerton and comedian Lee Mack discusses the return of his comedy series Not Going Out. With music from McFly, who perform their current single Tonight Is the Night

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens continue their first Drag Race UK tour. It's an all-ages show in Glasgow and Alyssa Edwards is moved, while Crystal's death-defying act causes a medical emergency for a member of the audience

programme.title: God Shave the Queens
programme.desc: The queens conclude their inaugural Drag Race UK tour as, on the final night, Secret Santa comes to town and it looks like she's bearing gifts for those participants who have been more naughty than nice

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of one man's claim for more than £300,000, when it reveals that he can do everything he claims he can't and there's 'paws for thought' as a pet owner refuses to believe her furry friend isn't covered by a pet insurance policy. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Two friends are rescued from shark infested seas, and petrol station workers in Birmingham save a woman from car jackers. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, four experts pool their talents to tackle the repair of a battered old ventriloquist's dummy, before silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a silver running trophy that survived a direct hit during the Blitz in the Second World War. Elsewhere, the experts turn their attention to a vintage portable typewriter and an old shove ha'penny board

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: MOBO Awards 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the 2020 MOBO Awards, a virtual celebration of black music and culture. This year's unique ceremony involves ten awards, two cultural films and nine unique performances from some of the biggest and most exciting names in music right now, all unified with a positive narrative of mobilisation after one of the most extraordinary years in modern history - from the global Covid-19 pandemic, to the public killing of George Floyd and the subsequent rise to prominence of the Black Lives Matter. Featuring performances by Headie One and M Huncho, Ms Banks, Tiwa Savage and Kojey Radical

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Sam, who has Down's syndrome, prepares for his debut in a public wrestling match. Marcus is enjoying success in the ring, and is pursuing romance outside it

programme.desc: The investigation into Ian's attack continues but as alibis are given, other information comes to light giving the police more leads. Kat is stunned when Jack tells her about Kush's visit to the police station. Jay continues to support Honey in the aftermath of her assault, and Suki tries to make an example of Will for shoplifting

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second group of four face two tough tests in a bid to make it through to finals week. First they receive a masterclass with Michelin-starred chef Andrew Wong, who demonstrates two dishes - dim sum and crispy chilli beef. They must then produce two dishes, emulating Andrew's techniques, with the judges' favourite receiving a pass through to next week. the remaining three must serve up a two-course signature menu, after which Monica Galetti, Gregg Wallace and Marcus Wareing will decide who else deserves a place in the final

programme.title: Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal
programme.desc: Tom Brook marks the fortieth anniversary of former Beatle John Lennon's murder in New York on 8 December 1980. His untimely death was a slaying that shocked millions of fans around the world. In Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal, the BBC's Tom Brook, the first British journalist to report live from the site of the New York shooting, takes stock of Lennon's life and the legacy of his music. Brook recounts what happened on the night Lennon died when he returned to his home, the Dakota apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with his wife Yoko Ono, and he hears from some of Lennon's closest confidantes as well as younger Lennon fans in America today. Drawing on a wealth of archive material, the programme includes Tom Brook's interview with the former Beatle's widow Yoko Ono, one of the first she gave, in which she discussed Lennon's life and death. The programme assesses John Lennon's powerful legacy 40 years on and asks whether his messages of love and peace are still re

programme.desc: Documentary following the story of Roly, a baby black rhino growing up on the African plains who has a lot to learn in its first year, from wallowing in mud to browsing. programme.title: Bill W
programme.desc: Time Magazine named Bill Wilson, the founder of Alcoholics Anonymous, one of its 100 Heroes and Icons of the 20th century for the legacy he left behind in helping millions find an answer not only to alcoholism, but to many other forms of addiction that destroy lives all around the world. The film follows Wilson through his 17 years of alcoholism, the events that led to his own sobriety, the crucial years of AA's founding and growth, his battle with depression, his experimentation with LSD, and his struggle with celebrity status in an anonymous society

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Victoria, Mel, Emma and Gerry pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet has some alarming news, Marlon has a good idea for Paddy, and Billy is annoyed with Dawn

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street's debut on ITV, the trial verdict leads to a dramatic showdown between Geoff and Alya. Meanwhile, Ken and Abi lead the charge against Ray's demolition, and Carla and Daniel inadvertently fuel Peter's paranoia

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Great British Escape
programme.desc: In a new six-part series, Paul O'Grady shares the beautiful sights, stories and secrets of his beloved adopted home county, Kent. In a year where many people have chosen to holiday closer to home due to the Covid-19 pandemic, Paul embraces the opportunity to rediscover what the county has to offer. This time, Paul explores Kent's beautiful forests and woodlands, visiting the Wildwood Trust outside Canterbury, a wildlife conservation centre protecting some of Britain's endangered animals. Later, he Paul to Kentish Pip, a 100-year old cider apple orchard, before heading deep into the weald of Kent to learn the ancient skill of horse coppicing

programme.title: Queens of the Street
programme.desc: A special programme celebrating the women who have starred in Coronation Street over the years, marking 60 years since the very first episode aired in December 1960. The programme features contributions from an array of the show's leading ladies - past and present - including Julie Hesmondhalgh, Sally Lindsay, Helen Worth, Alison King, Sue Nicholls, Sally Dynevor, Kate Ford, Dolly-Rose Campbell and Patti Clare, along with writers, producers and celebrity fans.

programme.title: Death Row's Women with Susanna Reid
programme.desc: The Good Morning Britain presenter travels to Texas, home of the most active death chamber in the United States, to meet death row inmate Darlie Routier. Twenty three years ago, the Dallas housewife was convicted and sentenced to death by legal injection for killing one of her sons, and remains accused of murdering the other - despite there being no eyewitnesses, no confession and no motive. Darlie denies the murders and decades on has refused a route off death row in exchange for accepting her guilt. Susanna delves into the history of the case, meeting key witnesses and family members, to find out if the convict is as innocent as she claims

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

At training, fitness coach Luke Worthington puts the boys through their paces, but it's not looking good as the team is carrying so many injuries there are only three fit players. programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Great British Escape
programme.desc: In a new six-part series, Paul O'Grady shares the beautiful sights, stories and secrets of his beloved adopted home county, Kent. In a year where many people have chosen to holiday closer to home due to the Covid-19 pandemic, Paul embraces the opportunity to rediscover what the county has to offer. This time, Paul explores Kent's beautiful forests and woodlands, visiting the Wildwood Trust outside Canterbury, a wildlife conservation centre protecting some of Britain's endangered animals. Later, he Paul to Kentish Pip, a 100-year old cider apple orchard, before heading deep into the weald of Kent to learn the ancient skill of horse coppicing

programme.title: Queens of the Street
programme.desc: A special programme celebrating the women who have starred in Coronation Street over the years, marking 60 years since the very first episode aired in December 1960. The programme features contributions from an array of the show's leading ladies - past and present - including Julie Hesmondhalgh, Sally Lindsay, Helen Worth, Alison King, Sue Nicholls, Sally Dynevor, Kate Ford, Dolly-Rose Campbell and Patti Clare, along with writers, producers and celebrity fans.

programme.title: Death Row's Women with Susanna Reid
programme.desc: The Good Morning Britain presenter travels to Texas, home of the most active death chamber in the United States, to meet death row inmate Darlie Routier. Twenty three years ago, the Dallas housewife was convicted and sentenced to death by legal injection for killing one of her sons, and remains accused of murdering the other - despite there being no eyewitnesses, no confession and no motive. Darlie denies the murders and decades on has refused a route off death row in exchange for accepting her guilt. Susanna delves into the history of the case, meeting key witnesses and family members, to find out if the convict is as innocent as she claims
